headline: Chevron’s Mediterranean Gas Push And What It Could Mean For CVX Valuation
summary: Chevron has started drilling at the Narges natural gas field in Egypt, expanding its Eastern Mediterranean footprint. The company has also secured a 20 year natural gas supply agreement through the Leviathan partnership in Israel. These projects are aimed at increasing regional production capacity and reinforcing Chevron’s role in Mediterranean gas markets. For investors tracking NYSE:CVX, these moves come as the stock trades around $191.43, with returns of 22.8% year to date and 44.7% over...

headline: SCHD’s Dividend Quality Filter Now Concentrates 42 Percent of Your Money in Just Ten Stocks
summary: Most investors buy Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (NYSEARCA:SCHD) because the fund’s reputation tells them they are buying diversification. SCHD has 105 holdings, screens hard on dividend quality, and charges 0.06%. The retiree who built an income sleeve around SCHD usually has not read the holdings list. headline: Intel, Exxon, and 10 More Stocks That Explain Today’s Market
summary: This breakneck tech rally still has legs—and it’s driving the stock market higher.  The tech rally was being driven by chip stocks again, as  Intel  climbed 2.8%,  Micron Technology  jumped 6%, and  Sandisk  added 3.3% ahead of the opening bell.  The on-again, off-again talks between the U.S. and Iran have led to oil prices sliding—and oil stocks were weighing on the S&P 500 and the Dow.
